data.body:
PASS-2 (PASS-3 verifier, findings 12–13) — independent re-grounding against the live ticket graph (children a36b, 5a6c) confirms both findings rest on the same inverted reading of child-ticket intent; neither is entailed.

[12] a36b ("worm-folly-barge") is a BUG whose title/description name "ANY new commit (even unrelated) invalidates a fresh attestation" as the DEFECT TO FIX, not a requirement the plan must honor. a36b AC1: "Claim freshness is scoped to the code the review was grounded against (resolved edit-set / cited files) rather than the whole HEAD sha, so an unrelated commit does NOT stale the attestation." Expected: "claim succeeds … none of the code … changed." That is the SAME material-only scoping the plan adopts — the plan AGREES with a36b, it does not narrow away from it. ANY-commit-invalidates survives only as the empty-set conservative FALLBACK in sibling 22bf (unscopable tickets), not a broad dropped requirement. Citation inverted. Duplicates prior PASS-2 finding [10] on this ticket.

[13] 5a6c ("grace-thud-feint") is a LIVE OPEN STORY under THIS epic (parent_id=4fcd; depends_on 22bf) titled "Narrowed delta-re-review on code drift…", carrying the full narrowed decision (re-run only {P2,P3} ∪ applicable CODEBASE_GROUNDED; reuse cached findings; chained attestation; fail-safe to full re-review on skew). Decomposition comment: "Story 2 grace-thud-feint (5a6c, depends_on S1) = narrowed delta-re-review." A refinement decomposed into a dependency-sequenced child is not silently dropped; the epic's 3 ACs are intentionally the minimal success contract. Duplicates prior PASS-2 finding [11].

cited_reference_accurate: [12]=no (a36b says the inverse of what the finding asserts); [13]=na (5a6c correctly named, but the dropped-inference is wrong — it is an open child of this epic).
